seoTitle: Alice Springs vs Alpurrurulam — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Alice Springs and Alpurrurulam. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Alice Springs vs Alpurrurulam: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Alice Springs and Alpurrurulam.
heading: Alice Springs vs Alpurrurulam — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Alice Springs, Northern Territory and Alpurrurulam, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Alice Springs has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Alpurrurulam (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Alice Springs or Alpurrurulam safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Alice Springs has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Alpurrurulam scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Alice Springs scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Alpurrurulam scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Alice Springs or Alpurrurulam?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Alice Springs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
